_provenance.design: working-surface re-run of experiments/designs/lexical-bridging-context-v1 (the lexical bridging-context probe v1)
_provenance.kind: trigger-(b)-style witness-seek, NOT a re-tune of the frozen v1
_provenance.what_changed: ONLY the output channel. Each framing's system prompt keeps the v1 TASK DESCRIPTION byte-identical and replaces ONLY the trailing answer-format clause: v1 forbade a working surface ('Answer with ... and nothing else'); this variant PERMITS step-by-step working and parses a 'FINAL:' tag. Plus the output channel is physically widened (max_tokens raised; see channel_change). Nothing else moves: same 88 items (same gitignored full-text files), same temperatures, same A sample count (5), same item classes, same per-axis reading rule, same Q3 control.
_provenance.what_did_NOT_change: Reasoning-effort knob HELD CONSTANT at the v1 setting (claude/gpt {"enabled": false}; google/gemini {"effort": "minimal"}, reasoning_tokens=0) — the working surface is opened ONLY in the VISIBLE output channel, exactly as result/relational-order-composition-c-reasoning-scaffold and result/scivetti-let-alone-working-surface-v1 did. So this is a pure output-channel manipulation, not a reasoning-budget change.
_provenance.format_only_enforced_by_construction: probe.py does NOT hand-copy the v1 task text. It LOADS the parent instrument.json, asserts each 'old' clause below is a verbatim suffix of v1's system string, and replaces ONLY that suffix. The task-description prefix is therefore byte-identical to v1 by construction. verify_format_only.py prints the longest-common-prefix per framing so a pre-run critic can audit that the ONLY diff is the answer-format clause.
_provenance.tests: the ungraded-COMMITMENT half of result/lexical-bridging-context-v1 (claim/lexical-graded-scale-ungraded-commitment). v1 read the commitment null off a constrained single-label channel; essay/output-channel-confound holds a forced-format negative is channel-bounded until the channel is varied. open-question/gradience-population-not-moment names THIS re-run as the discriminating test and the three-state grid (channel-bounded / channel-not-taken-up / channel-controlled) it resolves into. SCOPE CAVEAT (essay): the confound bites only where the probed handling is SERIAL and EXTERNALIZABLE; whether a same/different-sense commitment is that kind of computation is part of what this re-run reveals.
_provenance.uptake_clause: essay/output-channel-confound: a working surface is an affordance, not a coercion. 'vary the channel' means vary the channel ACTUALLY USED. probe.py records per-call completion_tokens, reasoning_tokens, content length, and whether a FINAL tag was emitted, so the analysis can report UPTAKE model-by-model and read a declined surface as channel-not-taken-up (inconclusive), never as a survival.

channel_change.v1_max_tokens: openrouter.call default: 64 for claude/gpt, 4096 for google
channel_change.this_variant_max_tokens: 2048 for claude/gpt (room to externalize working), 4096 for google (unchanged). Part of the output-channel widening; reasoning-effort UNCHANGED.
channel_change.parse_rule: extract the text after the LAST case-insensitive 'FINAL:' occurrence, then apply the v1 parse for that framing to that text. If no FINAL tag is present, fall back to parsing the whole content (flagged no_tag=true, an uptake/format miss).

framing_answer_clause_replacements._note: Each 'old' is the VERBATIM trailing answer-format clause of the v1 framing's system string (parent_instrument). probe.py asserts old is a suffix of v1's string before replacing it with new. 'new' is purely an output-format instruction — it introduces NO new task content, NO new scale, NO new option, NO new threshold.

b_rel.old: Answer with a single integer from 0 to 100 and nothing else.
b_rel.new:
Think step by step about the two uses, then give your rating on a final line in the form:
FINAL: <a single integer from 0 to 100>

b_conf.old: Answer with exactly one line in the form: SAME 87  or  DIFFERENT 40 — the word SAME or DIFFERENT, a space, then an integer 0-100, and nothing else.
b_conf.new:
Think step by step about the two uses, then give your decision and confidence on a final line in the form:
FINAL: SAME 87   or   FINAL: DIFFERENT 40 — the word SAME or DIFFERENT, a space, then an integer 0-100.

c_third.old: Answer with exactly one word: SAME, DIFFERENT, or UNCLEAR — and nothing else.
c_third.new:
Think step by step about the two uses, then give your answer on a final line in the form:
FINAL: SAME   or   FINAL: DIFFERENT   or   FINAL: UNCLEAR

topic.old: Answer with a single integer from 0 to 100 and nothing else.
topic.new:
Think step by step, then give your rating on a final line in the form:
FINAL: <a single integer from 0 to 100>

a_forced.old: Answer with exactly one word: SAME or DIFFERENT — and nothing else.
a_forced.new:
Think step by step about the two uses, then give your answer on a final line in the form:
FINAL: SAME   or   FINAL: DIFFERENT

reading_rule: IDENTICAL to v1 (instrument.json -> reading_rule_frozen): position certified by B/b_rel alone; confidence/dispersion by BOTH B/b_conf and C/c_third. The witness-seek question is COMPARATIVE: does the v1 ungraded-COMMITMENT null (bridging confidence high, decline ~0, dispersion ~0) shift toward GRADED commitment under the wide channel? analyze.py reports the same per-axis verdict AND a head-to-head delta vs v1's analysis.json, plus the uptake report.

cost_control.calls_per_item_per_model: same as v1: B(b_rel+b_conf)+C(c_third)+Q3(topic)=4 temp-0 + A(a_forced)=5 temp-1.0 = 9; 9 x 88 x 3 = 2376.
cost_control.note: A working surface raises completion tokens, claude the driver (v1 claude $0.449 of $0.756 on SHORT outputs). NEXT.md flags a prior working-surface run hit ~$1.58. Do a MEASURED per-model calibration before the full run; if the single-shot estimate exceeds the $2.50 single-run flag, drop the A dispersion read (characterizing-only, 1320 of the 2376 calls; the instrument's own cost_control sanctions dropping A — it does not touch the verdict). gemini effort stays minimal.
